skill.name: react-flow-best-practices
skill.description: Best practices for writing, reviewing, and refactoring React Flow / @xyflow/react v12 canvases. Use when work touches React Flow components, custom nodes, custom edges, handles, node data typing, viewport controls, MiniMap/Background/Panel usage, NodeToolbar, NodeResizer, or workflow-builder review tasks.
skill.summary: Review and improve React Flow implementations with v12 patterns for nodes, edges, handles, and viewport behavior.
skill.author: JohnWayneeee

security_audit.summary: All 247 static findings are false positives. The scanner misidentified Markdown inline code backticks as Ruby/shell backtick execution, common documentation words as weak cryptographic algorithms, and React Flow documentation text as system reconnaissance. The skill contains only benign React Flow best-practice documentation with no executable code, no network requests, and no malicious intent.

content.value_statement: React Flow implementations often break when node IDs drift, handle contracts mismatch, or viewport behavior fights user interaction. This skill provides concrete review patterns for @xyflow/react v12 so you can catch correctness, performance, and accessibility issues before they reach production.
actual_capabilities: Review React Flow canvas architecture and state ownership
Check node and edge type contracts for stability
Validate handle IDs and dynamic handle updates
Audit viewport behavior and accessibility settings
Identify performance issues like inline nodeTypes or excessive subscriptions
Review Next.js client boundaries and CSS imports
limitations: Does not generate or run code automatically
Requires the user to provide source files for review
Focused on @xyflow/react v12; older versions may differ
Cannot verify runtime behavior without execution

use_cases.title: Code review for workflow builders
use_cases.description: A frontend engineer uses the skill to review a React Flow canvas before merging a pull request, catching handle mismatches and viewport bugs early.

use_cases.title: Refactor legacy React Flow code
use_cases.description: A developer upgrades an older React Flow implementation to v12 and uses the skill to verify type safety, provider boundaries, and state ownership.

use_cases.title: Accessibility audit for canvas products
use_cases.description: A product team reviews keyboard navigation, focus styles, and aria labels in a workflow canvas to meet accessibility requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Quick canvas review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my React Flow canvas for common issues with nodes, edges, and viewport behavior. Here is the component code: [paste code]
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user wants a fast sanity check on a single canvas component

prompt_templates.title: Check handle contracts
prompt_templates.prompt: Check if my custom nodes and edges have matching handle IDs. Review these files: [paste node and edge code]
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer suspects edge connections are breaking due to handle mismatches

prompt_templates.title: Performance audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my React Flow implementation for performance issues like inline nodeTypes, unnecessary re-renders, or full-array subscriptions. Here is the code: [paste code]
prompt_templates.scenario: Canvas slows down as the workflow grows

prompt_templates.title: Full workflow refactor review
prompt_templates.prompt: I am refactoring a large workflow builder to @xyflow/react v12. Review the architecture for state ownership, type contracts, custom components, accessibility, and viewport behavior. Here are the key files: [paste files]
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user upgrading a complex workflow application

output_examples.input: Review this React Flow component for issues: [component with inline nodeTypes and missing nodrag on buttons]
output: - High: App.tsx:42 - nodeTypes is created inline inside the render function. This causes React Flow to remount custom nodes on every render.
- Medium: WorkflowNode.tsx:15 - Button inside the node lacks nodrag, so clicks can start dragging the node instead of triggering the action.
- Low: Canvas.tsx:8 - Parent container uses height: 100% but no ancestor defines a height, so the canvas may be invisible.
1. Move nodeTypes outside the component or memoize it with useMemo.
2. Add className="nodrag nopan" to buttons and inputs inside custom nodes.
3. Set an explicit height on the canvas parent container.

output_examples.input: Check if my edges and handles match: [node with handles and edge definitions]
output: - Edge e-1 references sourceHandle: source-style, but StylePreviewNode renders Handle id=source-preview. These do not match, so the edge will not connect visually.
- Update the edge sourceHandle to source-preview, or change the node handle ID to source-style. Keep IDs stable after users save flows.

best_practices: Define nodeTypes and edgeTypes outside the component or memoize them to prevent remounts
Protect interactive controls inside nodes with nodrag, nopan, and nowheel classes
Type custom nodes and edges with specific NodeProps and EdgeProps contracts instead of any
anti_patterns: Creating nodeTypes inline inside a component render function
Calling fitView in effects that depend on full nodes arrays, which fights user pan and zoom
Subscribing many node components to the full nodes or edges arrays instead of targeted hooks

faq.question: Which React Flow version does this skill target?
faq.answer: The skill targets @xyflow/react v12. Older versions may have different APIs and behavior.

faq.question: Does the skill generate code or only review it?
faq.answer: The skill reviews and suggests improvements. It does not generate or execute code automatically.

faq.question: What files should I share for a useful review?
faq.answer: Share the React Flow canvas component, custom node and edge components, and any state management or store files that handle nodes and edges.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Next.js projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill checks for client component boundaries, global CSS imports, and provider placement specific to Next.js.

faq.question: Why are my edges not connecting to handles?
faq.answer: The most common cause is a mismatch between edge sourceHandle or targetHandle values and the actual Handle id values rendered on the node. The skill checks these contracts.

faq.question: How do I prevent the canvas from refitting on every data change?
faq.answer: Avoid calling fitView in effects that depend on the full nodes or edges array. Use a userInteractedRef to track manual viewport changes and gate auto-fit behavior.
